What travelers are saying

  • viviana cl
    Buenos Aires, Argentina198 contributions

    Amazing! not to be missed!

    The history and the beautiful architecture is not to be missed! You need to prebook the ticket online and pick a time to visit. Make sure you arrive on time because the slots are every ten minutes and the staff moves you to visit the rest once you finish your ten minutes at the main room. The booking can be made either to visit on your own, using an audio guide in the language of your choosing or there are 2 visits are day with an Italian guide or an English guide. The tour with audioguide costs 12 euros and the one with the tour guide costs 14 euros.
    Review of: Archiginnasio Di Bologna
    Written April 17, 2026
  • roberto p
    Gallarate, Italy2,869 contributions

    To spend the whole day there

    One of the most attractive places in Bologna.

    First of all for the location, in Piazza Maggiore.

    The largest library in the city, large, airy, always full of students and youth, but also "ordinary people" who consult books and - upstairs - all kinds of newspapers and periodicals.

    And then the Etruscan archaeological excavations, freely accessible, as well as the entrance to the library.

    We also spend the whole day there willingly
    Review of: Biblioteca Salaborsa
    Automatically translated
    Written November 20, 2025
  • vedegheto
    Milan, Italy114 contributions

    Very interesting but badly organized

    Surely the Archiginnasio is a beautiful place and worth a visit. The ticket to access the only rooms of the Stabat Mater that are on the first floor and that cannot be visited for free (cost 10 euro full and 8 reduced), or the hall of the Stabat Mater and that of the anatomic table, can be purchased exclusively online. Once in a lifetime this visit is definitely worth it and the audio guide is useful and balanced support. So the structure (and also the audio guide) also deserve a 5, but the organization (or disorganization?) and the staff determine the lowering of judgment.
    Review of: Archiginnasio Di Bologna
    Automatically translated
    Written May 5, 2026
  • Gee240525
    Medway, UK213 contributions

    Interesting historical library with Roman excavations underneath

    The library is located Piazza del Nettuno and we found it easy to identify by the number of young people sat on the steps outside. It is completely free to enter and is a stunning building. From what I read online and from some of the images around the main hall, it used to be a basketball court and the entrance hall was a telegraph exchange. It now looks like a library that is accommodating of everyone. We saw an area we believe was a creche, there was a well attended cafe with good prices to one side and also an art exhibition going on in the main area. The main hall has a glass floor through which you can view excavations.
    This was our main reason for visiting. If you follow the signs (or if you don't see them, enter the main hall and take the stairs immediately to your left) You'll end up in the basement. I think you then turn right and there is a hallway with a friendly gentleman who asked our nationality. Entrance to the excavations is free but donations are welcome. You'll walk through an area with information (in Italian) about the library building going back in time and pass a large vault style door (we believe this area was once part of the bank). At the end of the corridor you enter the excavations, which you walk through and above on a metal walkway. The signs are in both Italian and English so you can read about what you are seeing. There are a couple of wells and some streets that are easy to make out. I think there was also a cistern. The earliest parts I believe are from the 8th or 9th century. If you happen to look up you can see the feet of the people stood in the main hall above you. It's really interesting. and well worth visiting.
    Review of: Biblioteca Salaborsa
    Written October 27, 2025
  • Jasmina D
    185 contributions

    Poor organization

    I am truly sorry to have to award only 3 stars to this majestic piece of history. What I saw was of an indescribable beauty, but all ruined by bad organization. We enter from the right staircase and, not having the ticket in hand, we agree in the line "tickets to buy". There were already people queuing up, and more join behind us. A single attendant goes back and forth and lets only those with the ticket already purchased online pass through (so queue in the row next to us). After more than 10 minutes he approaches our queue and lets only 3 people pass by (one of them very old) and gives him the ticket at the desk. Another 10 min wait and in the meantime a lot of people with tickets already purchased online enter. Finally, very annoyed, she looks at the copious line that has formed on our side and tells us that today you only enter with the ticket pre-purchased online. We all look at her in amazement and then she adds "eh, I can't make the tickets too I'm here alone". That's crazy. There were 2 separate rows of which one x buy tickets. Nowhere did it say you only entered with a pre-purchased ticket. That was his decision of the moment. People with booked tours who didn't know where to go. No indication. Finally we make the tickets online and get in. The audio guide is a qr code to download at the entrance. Let's take our little ride. At the end of the visit we linger a little near the library and we see the people who climb the other staircase (the one theoretically reserved for the exit) and enter quietly without paying the ticket that I -after 25 minutes of unnecessary waiting at the ticket office- bought for me and all my family members. I would say that the 3 stars I awarded are too many.... and I reiterate that this place is simply great.....
    Review of: Archiginnasio Di Bologna
    Automatically translated
    Written December 29, 2025
  • Noraatc
    Sudbury, MA44,365 contributions

    The Urban Center of Bologna.

    Biblioteca Salaborsa is the main public library of Bologna located in the Palazzo d'Accursio on the Piazza del Nettuno. One of the attractions is the archaeological site visible through the crystal floor in the centre of the library.

    I was more attracted by the impressive hall, the Urban Center of Bologna, with its beautiful ceiling.

    The entrance to the Biblioteca Salaborsa is free.
    Review of: Biblioteca Salaborsa
    Written May 18, 2025
  • ShaunH290
    London, UK3,484 contributions

    Bologna the Learned's Home!

    The Archiginnasio di Bologna was the original attempt to bring the faculties and buildings of the world's oldest university into one place. Though founded in 1088, possibly only eight years before Oxford, by building this "palace" in 1562-3, Pope Pius III simplified things until it ceased to be a University in 1803 when the University was moved to Palazzo Poggi where it is today.
    The building contains various parts that are still enjoyed by tourists today, including the walls are ceilings around the central courtyard with its multitude of coats of arms, its libraries, the Stabat Mater Hall and the famous anatomy theatre, which was only used in January and February to ensure that it was cold enough to be comfortable with cadavers on the table. Note that some of these need to be paid for separately and booked - so you need some planning, especially at busy times of year. We were fine in February.
    Review of: Archiginnasio Di Bologna
    Written January 30, 2026
  • Oana N
    Bucharest, Romania378 contributions

    A beautiful building used wisely

    If I were to choose my favorite place in Bologna I would say that this is the one! I love this space! I find’T have time to discover it completely but I appreciate a beautiful building, with a spectacular ceiling. I loved the fact that there were people from all ages, it was a space full with strollers, people enjoying the space and different activities I would love to return and to discover it all
    Review of: Biblioteca Salaborsa
    Written January 19, 2026
  • GiuliaA.95
    17 contributions

    Password: DAMN IT!

    Our visit was seriously compromised by the unacceptable attitude found at the ticket office. The cashier greeted us with extremely rude manners and unjustifiably aggressive tones, despite the fact that there was no responsibility or improper behavior on our part, except that we had queued up for the 10-minute shift before ours (obviously, for misunderstanding and certainly not to pass in front of anyone. We are on vacation and certainly not in a hurry!).
    Such a level of rudeness is unacceptable in public service and denotes a total lack of professionalism. We believe it is essential that what has happened be dealt with very seriously, so that such incidents do not happen again.
    Review of: Archiginnasio Di Bologna
    Automatically translated
    Written December 29, 2025
  • GoldUS
    48 contributions

    Etruscan Ruins

    Worth the visit just for the Etruscan Ruins beneath the library. Accessible down a flight of stairs as soon as you enter the main entrance (to your left). Free entrance and a nice 20-30 minute activity.
    Review of: Biblioteca Salaborsa
    Written September 14, 2025
  • Alessandra T
    1 contribution

    Rude and unprepared staff

    Bad and disappointing experience.
    We went yesterday afternoon, around 5pm, to the Archiginnasio and the staff proved rude, unprepared and totally inadequate.
    We were given the wrong information, which led us to buy the wrong tickets. When we asked for clarification or guidance on a possible refund, a local blonde lady was extremely rude, unprofessional and treated us with mockery, without giving us any useful information or showing herself available.
    No willingness to help, no explanation, just arrogant and disrespectful attitude.
    The only positive note was a guide, who on the contrary was kind and competent, trying to remedy a situation created by the disorganization of the rest of the staff.
    A place of great historical value ruined by poor reception and poor professionalism. Experience that does not tempt you to return.
    Review of: Archiginnasio Di Bologna
    Automatically translated
    Written December 30, 2025
  • David
    Suria, Spain367 contributions

    Worth getting in

    The Salaborsa Library is the main public library of Bologna located in the Piazza del Nettuno. On the lower floor are the remains of an archaeological excavation and can be seen through the glass floor. In summer it can serve as a refuge from the heat. The ceiling is fabulous and what surprised me was that they have a room to listen to vinyls with exceptional quality.
    Review of: Biblioteca Salaborsa
    Automatically translated
    Written June 16, 2025
  • Simonpee17
    Vienna, Austria3,250 contributions

    Place rich in history, to be explored with a guide

    I visited the Archiginnasio in Bologna during my stay in the city. It is a fascinating place rich in history: it was the first permanent seat of the University of Bologna since the sixteenth century and still retains the charm of the ancient institution, with walls entirely decorated with student coats of arms and inscriptions.

    The paid part includes only two rooms: the Stabat Mater Hall and the famous Anatomical Theatre, certainly the most suggestive point of the visit. Many other areas of the palace are open to visitors without a ticket.

    Personally, having chosen only the audio guide, I would not recommend it as the only option: explanations are essential and I believe that with a live guide the experience would be much more engaging. Overall an interesting visit, but I recommend evaluating the guided tour to really appreciate it at its best.
    Review of: Archiginnasio Di Bologna
    Automatically translated
    Written February 22, 2026
  • Noraatc
    Sudbury, MA44,365 contributions

    The Temple of Knowledge

    I had been to Bologna many years ago, but I didn’t forget the beauty of the Archiginnasio with its charming courtyard and magnificent elaborate frescoes of the Portico del Pavaglione, so I promptly returned to see it again on the first day of my stay in the city.
    My visit fell on a weekend and the tickets for the Teatro Anatomico and Stabat Mater Hall were sold out by the end of the day, but I
    enjoyed the exhibit of exquisite flower arrangements created by the members of the Garden Club of Bologna, which was not on display on a weekday, when I returned to the Archiginnasio.

    My visit on a weekday was a completely different story: no crowds and the tickets to the Teatro Anatomico and Sala Stabat Mater (3€) were readily available. I had been to both before, but who would say no to the temple of science the mid-17th century teatro anatomico? And what can say about its gorgeous interior carved in wood? And the two famous “Spellati” (“skinned”) sculptures with every muscle carved with precision… Luckily, there were no events held in the Sala Stabat Mater, so I was able to see the collection of science books and take a glimpse at the Sale Historiche (Historic Rooms). through the wrought iron door.

    Both sights were completely mobbed over the weekend … twenty people max on a weekday and no line to buy the tickets.
    Review of: Archiginnasio Di Bologna
    Written May 19, 2025
  • viviana cl
    Buenos Aires, Argentina198 contributions

    Amazing! not to be missed!

    The history and the beautiful architecture is not to be missed! You need to prebook the ticket online and pick a time to visit. The tour with audioguide costs 12 euros and the one with the tour guide costs 14 euros.
